choices.text: Very high Isp (2,500-4,000s ion, 1,500-3,000s Hall) but very low thrust (50-300 mN). Builds delta-v over months/years. Perfect for deep space (Dawn, BepiColombo) and stationkeeping (Starlink Hall thrusters). Useless for launch.

questions.explanation: High Isp + low thrust = slow but efficient.

choices.text: Nuclear thermal (Isp ~900s, high thrust) in development (NASA DRACO, DARPA). 2× chemical Isp halves fuel needed for Mars round-trip. NERVA tested 1960s; reviving 2025+. Or chemical + electric hybrid.

questions.explanation: Nuclear thermal = high thrust + good Isp.
